# qbuem-db

C++23 DB driver library built on qbuem-stack.
Async ORM + PostgreSQL (async) · MySQL/MariaDB (cloud) · SQLite3 · Redis (async) drivers + driver-independent migration framework.

## File Structure

- `src/db/orm.hpp`                    — Header-only generic ORM (C++23 Concepts)
- `src/db/postgresql_driver.hpp/cpp`  — Async PostgreSQL driver (libpq non-blocking)
- `src/db/sqlite3_driver.hpp/cpp`     — SQLite3 amalgamation driver (dev / test)
- `src/db/mysql_driver.hpp/cpp`       — MySQL/MariaDB driver (libmysqlclient, cloud SQL)
- `src/db/redis_client.hpp/cpp`       — Pure RESP2 async Redis client (no external deps)
- `src/db/migration/migrator.hpp`     — Header-only driver-independent migration framework
- `CMakeLists.txt`                    — Build config (FetchContent + ExternalProject + find_library)

## CMake Targets

- `qbuem-db::orm`               — Header-only ORM (INTERFACE)
- `qbuem-db::migration`         — Header-only migration (INTERFACE)
- `qbuem-db::sqlite3_driver`    — SQLite3 driver (STATIC)
- `qbuem-db::postgresql_driver` — PostgreSQL async driver (STATIC)
- `qbuem-db::mysql_driver`      — MySQL/MariaDB driver (STATIC, requires system libmysqlclient)
- `qbuem-db::redis_client`      — Redis async client (STATIC)

## ORM API

Dialect-aware: PostgreSQL (`$N` / `RETURNING *`), MySQL (`?` / `ON DUPLICATE KEY`), SQLite (`?` / `ON CONFLICT EXCLUDED`).

```cpp
// Registration (once at startup)
orm::register_table<T>("table_name")
    .dialect(Dialect::MySQL)    // optional — default PostgreSQL
    .pk("id", &T::id)
    .col("col", &T::col);

// SQL generation
auto& m = orm::meta<T>();
m.sql_select_where("col")              // SELECT … WHERE col=$1
m.sql_select_where_in("col", n)        // SELECT … WHERE col IN ($1,…,$n)
m.sql_select_where_null("col")         // SELECT … WHERE col IS NULL
m.sql_select_where_between("age")      // SELECT … WHERE age BETWEEN $1 AND $2
m.sql_select_where_like("name")        // SELECT … WHERE name LIKE $1
m.sql_select_where_paged("col",1,2,3)  // WHERE col=$1 LIMIT $2 OFFSET $3
m.sql_count_where("col")               // SELECT COUNT(*) … WHERE col=$1
m.sql_count_where_in("col", n)
m.sql_select_where2("a","b")           // WHERE a=$1 AND b=$2
m.sql_insert()                         // INSERT … VALUES (…) [RETURNING *]
m.sql_insert_batch(n)                  // INSERT … VALUES (…),(…),…
m.sql_upsert_pk()                      // dialect-selected upsert
m.sql_update_pk()                      // UPDATE … SET … WHERE pk=$N
m.sql_delete_pk()                      // DELETE … WHERE pk=$1

// Parameter binding
m.bind_insert(obj)                     // non-PK values
m.bind_update(obj)                     // non-PK first, PK last
m.bind_batch(span<T>)                  // flat params for batch INSERT
m.bind_pk(obj)                         // PK only
m.bind_val(v) / bind_val2(a,b) / bind_val3(a,b,c)
m.bind_paged(where_val, limit, offset)
m.bind_in(vector<int64_t>{1,2,3})      // IN clause (arbitrary range)

// Result reading
T obj = m.read_row(*row);              // column-name based
T obj = m.read_row_indexed(*row);      // index-based (faster)
```

## Driver DSN

- PostgreSQL: `postgresql://user:pass@host:5432/db`
- SQLite3:    `sqlite:///path/to/file.db`  /  `sqlite://:memory:`
- MySQL:      `mysql://user:pass@host:3306/db[?ssl=true]`
- Redis:      `redis://[:pass@]host[:port][/db]`

## PostgreSQL Async Architecture

libpq non-blocking mode + qbuem Reactor integration.
- `PgFlushAwaiter`: yields on `PQflush==1`, resumes on socket writable event.
- `PgReadAwaiter`: yields on `PQisBusy==true`, resumes on readable event via `Reactor::post(resume)`.
- `PgAcquireAwaiter`: yields when pool is exhausted; woken by `release()`.
- Binary protocol (`resultFormat=1`), big-endian deserialization via `std::byteswap` (C++23).

## Redis Async Architecture

Pure POSIX TCP + RESP2 protocol. No external dependencies.
- `RedisWriteAwaiter`: non-blocking write; yields on writable event if incomplete.
- `RedisReadAwaiter`: yields on readable event until `RespParser` has a complete response.

Supported commands: GET/SET/DEL/EXISTS/EXPIRE/TTL/INCR/DECR/MGET/MSET (strings),
HGET/HSET/HDEL/HGETALL/HKEYS/HVALS (hash),
LPUSH/RPUSH/LPOP/RPOP/LRANGE/LLEN (list),
SADD/SREM/SMEMBERS/SCARD/SISMEMBER (set),
ZADD/ZREM/ZRANGE/ZRANGEBYSCORE/ZSCORE/ZCARD/ZRANK (sorted set),
TYPE/KEYS/SCAN/RENAME/PERSIST/PING/FLUSHDB/INFO + generic `command(args)`.

## MySQL Driver Architecture

libmysqlclient C API. Auto-converts `$N` → `?` placeholders.
Multi-connection pool, `MYSQL_STMT` prepared statements, UTF8MB4, TLS/SSL.
Cloud-compatible: RDS / Aurora / Cloud SQL / Azure DB / PlanetScale / TiDB.

## Migration Framework

History stored in `__schema_migrations` table. Driver-independent (specify placeholder style).
API: `migrate()` / `migrate_to(ver)` / `rollback()` / `rollback_to(ver)` / `status()`.
Supports multiple SQL statements separated by `;`. Atomic application within transaction.

## Dependencies

- **qbuem-stack**: FetchContent GIT (interfaces, Reactor, Task, Result)
- **SQLite3 3.49.1**: FetchContent ZIP, compiled directly
- **libpq 17.2**: ExternalProject_Add source build (libpq only)
- **libmysqlclient**: system `find_library` (optional, `apt install libmysqlclient-dev`)
